data.text: Attention, splashers. Due to a medical emergency, we are experiencing delays on Devil Dog's Drop. Thank you for your patience. I'm calling it. Time of death, 2.21 p.m. I can't believe this is happening. He was fine just a minute ago. It's now your fault. You had to be in his mid-80s. A guy his age shouldn't be climbing all those stairs, especially if he had a weak heart. Sometimes these things just happen, and, you know, look, I know I'm just an EMT, but maybe we should all take a moment of silence. Definitely. Yeah. Absolutely. All right, let's bow our heads. Okay, good. Uh... So I guess we should bring him down now. Uh-huh. Yes. Yep. And we should carry him down the stairs. Yes. That would be the right thing to do. Yeah. It's a lot of stairs. A lot of stairs on the way up here, right? Yes, 255. Ooh, 255. Is that really? Wow. Crazy what they can build these days, you know? I'm sorry. Can you guys, like, take him away? I'm kind of freaking out. Oh. Of course. Right, yeah. You hate to carry him past all these kids, though, you know? Could be really upsetting for them. That's a good point, Neil. That's actually an amazing point. Right? I just wish there was some other way... To get him down. No, are you serious? I didn't say anything. Did you say anything? I didn't say anything. Did you say anything? I'm sorry, what are you guys talking about? They want to send the dead guy down the water slide. Wait. Wait. Do you think we should send... him down the water slide. That is interesting. I had not considered that, but now that she brings it up, she's not wrong. I didn't bring it up. You two were clearly thinking about it. What? No. No. Our plan was to carry him down the 255 stairs. Yeah, right. And then, you know, she pitched the water slide idea. I mean, yeah, it's a good idea, Mary. I like that. I mean, he did wait in this long line, you know. Seems like he really wanted to go down it. It actually seems respectful. Okay, it wasn't my idea. And in what world is this respectful? At the bottom of the slide, you come out of a hole that's shaped like a dog's butt, and it takes a picture of you. What would his family think? We just wouldn't buy the picture. Yeah, we're like 15 bucks anyway. It's a total rip-off. Okay, but if y'all do end up wanting a picture, though, I could hook that up for free. My boy Dante, he work in the photo hut. Oh, that'd be great. Thank you. Stop helping them. What is wrong with you guys? All right. Hey, we're all in this together. Everyone's been through a lot today. I think the best thing to do is take a breath, relax, and vote on it. So, all in favor of our idea to carry him gently down the stairs, raise your hand. Okay, so one vote for that. Now, raise your hand if you want to do her idea and push the body down the slide. Yeah? Wow. So that's three of us, and then how many kids have the hands up? Oh, wow. Kids have the hands up? It's all the kids. Fair is fair. Mary, you won the vote. Congrats. This is a person. He lived a life. And you're going to push his body down a water slide because, what, it's more convenient? Yes. Yeah. Also, I want to see how big the splash is. Well, don't say that, man. That's not helping. I mean, I just want to see if the splash is different. We all do, but let's not say it. This was a human being. Exactly. And that's why you can't do this. You know what? She's right. We can't just send this man down the slide. Thank you. Somebody got to go with him. It's a double slide, so you need the weight to get through the loop. Oh, shotgun. Shotgun. Okay. I'm going to run down and film it. Oh, okay. Have fun. All right. All right. All right. Just go ahead and do arms crossed, legs crossed. Y'all go ahead and have fun.
